actors.name: User
actors.description: End-user interacting with the Superfluid protocol, initiating transactions like wrapping tokens, creating/managing streams, deploying contracts, and interacting with dApps. Referenced in doc_2, doc_13, doc_44.

actors.name: Super App
actors.description: Smart contracts registered with the Superfluid Protocol that react to Superfluid agreement events (e.g., stream creation/update/deletion) via callbacks. Referenced in doc_3, doc_21, doc_22, doc_24.
capabilities: Execute custom logic on stream events
Interact with Superfluid Host for callbacks

actors.name: Sentinel
actors.description: Off-chain nodes that monitor the Superfluid network for critical or insolvent streams and initiate liquidation transactions. Referenced in doc_1, doc_6, doc_18, doc_19.

actors.name: Superfluid DAO
actors.description: The decentralized autonomous organization responsible for governing the Superfluid protocol, including upgrades and parameter changes. Referenced in doc_11, doc_41.

actors.name: Pool Admin
actors.description: An actor responsible for creating and managing Superfluid Distribution Pools, including assigning units to pool members. Referenced in doc_5, doc_28, doc_30.

actors.name: Pool Member
actors.description: An actor who is a recipient in a Superfluid Distribution Pool, receiving tokens based on their allocated units. Referenced in doc_5, doc_28, doc_30.

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Individuals or teams building applications, Super Apps, or custom Super Tokens on top of the Superfluid protocol. Referenced in doc_13, doc_22, doc_26.
capabilities: Develop smart contracts interacting with Superfluid

actors.name: PIC (Patrician in Charge)
actors.description: An actor who participates in the Transparent Ongoing Auction (TOGA) to become the designated liquidator for a specific token, earning rewards for closing critical streams during the patrician period. Referenced in doc_1, doc_18.
capabilities: Receive rewards for liquidating streams during patrician period

assets.name: Super Token
assets.description: Programmable tokens (e.g., USDCx, DAIx, ETHx) that are ERC20 compatible and enable Superfluid functionalities like money streaming and distributions. Can be wrapped from ERC20s/native assets or be pure. Referenced in doc_1, doc_2, doc_7, doc_39.

assets.name: ERC20 Token
assets.description: Standard ERC20 tokens (e.g., USDC, DAI) that can be wrapped into Super Tokens to gain Superfluid functionalities. Referenced in doc_7, doc_14, doc_55.

assets.name: Native Asset
assets.description: Native blockchain assets (e.g., ETH, MATIC) that can be wrapped into Native Super Tokens. Referenced in doc_39, doc_55.

assets.name: Superfluid NFT
assets.description: Non-Fungible Tokens used within the Superfluid ecosystem, such as Tradeable Cashflow NFTs, ConstantOutflowNFTs, PoolAdminNFTs, representing ownership or rights related to streams or pools. Referenced in doc_3, doc_24, doc_32, doc_61.

assets.name: User Data
assets.description: Arbitrary byte data that can be attached to Superfluid operations (e.g., stream creation, updates) for custom logic or off-chain processing. Referenced in doc_1, doc_21, doc_37, doc_51.

assets.name: Buffer Deposit
assets.description: Super Tokens locked by a stream sender in the protocol when a stream is created, serving as a security deposit to ensure stream solvency. Referenced in doc_1, doc_6, doc_18.

assets.name: TOGA Stake
assets.description: Super Tokens staked by a Patrician in Charge (PIC) in the Transparent Ongoing Auction (TOGA) contract as a commitment to perform liquidations. Referenced in doc_1, doc_18.

assets.name: Private Keys
assets.description: Cryptographic keys used by actors (Users, Sentinels, Developers, etc.) to authorize transactions and interact with the blockchain. Referenced in doc_13, doc_19.

components.name: Superfluid Host Contract
components.description: The central UUPS proxy contract of the Superfluid protocol. It manages Superfluid Agreements, Super App registrations and callbacks, and overall protocol logic. Referenced in doc_1, doc_4, doc_23, doc_45, doc_61.

components.name: Super Token Contract
components.description: An instance of a UUPS proxy contract for each specific Super Token (e.g., fDAIx). It implements ERC20/ERC777 standards and Superfluid-specific logic like real-time balance calculation and interaction with agreements. Referenced in doc_7, doc_39, doc_59.

components.name: Constant Flow Agreement (CFAv1) Contract
components.description: A UUPS proxy contract, a Superfluid Agreement that manages the logic for creating, updating, deleting, and accounting for money streams (continuous token flows). Referenced in doc_1, doc_21, doc_61, doc_63.

components.name: General Distribution Agreement (GDAv1) Contract
components.description: A UUPS proxy contract, a Superfluid Agreement that manages the logic for distribution pools, allowing one-to-many or many-to-many token distributions (instant or streamed). Referenced in doc_28, doc_29, doc_61, doc_63.

components.name: Super Token Factory Contract
components.description: A UUPS proxy contract used to deploy new Super Token contracts (both wrapper and pure types). Referenced in doc_41, doc_42, doc_61.

components.name: CFAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable contract providing a simplified and gas-efficient interface for client-side applications to interact with the Constant Flow Agreement (e.g., creating/updating/deleting streams). Referenced in doc_10, doc_44, doc_50, doc_56.

components.name: GDAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable contract providing a simplified interface for client-side applications to interact with the General Distribution Agreement (e.g., creating pools, managing members). Referenced in doc_10, doc_44, doc_46, doc_57.

components.name: Sentinel Node Software
components.description: Off-chain software run by Sentinels to monitor Superfluid streams, identify critical/insolvent ones, and trigger liquidation transactions. Referenced in doc_1, doc_18, doc_19.

components.name: Superfluid Dashboard
components.description: A web-based user interface that allows users to interact with the Superfluid protocol, manage streams, pools, wrap/unwrap tokens, and view account activity. Referenced in doc_2, doc_16, doc_26, doc_53, doc_64.

components.name: Superfluid Subgraph
components.description: A data indexing service (using The Graph protocol) that provides a queryable API for accessing historical and real-time data from the Superfluid protocol. Referenced in doc_15, doc_20, doc_48, doc_52, doc_54, doc_62.

components.name: Vesting Scheduler Contract
components.description: A smart contract designed for setting up token vesting schedules with optional cliffs, initiating Superfluid streams for linear vesting. Referenced in doc_17, doc_64.

components.name: MacroForwarder Contract
components.description: A trusted forwarder contract that allows users to execute predefined batch operations (macros) on the Superfluid protocol in a single transaction. Referenced in doc_45, doc_49.

components.name: UserDefinedMacro Contract
components.description: A user-deployed smart contract that defines a specific sequence of Superfluid operations to be executed as a batch via the MacroForwarder. Referenced in doc_45.

components.name: SuperfluidPool Contract Instance
components.description: An instance of a UUPS proxy contract representing a specific distribution pool, created via the GDAv1. It holds pool-specific data like members and units. Referenced in doc_60, doc_61.

components.name: TOGA (Transparent Ongoing Auction) Contract
components.description: A smart contract that manages the auction for becoming the Patrician in Charge (PIC) for a given token, and handles staking and slashing for PICs. Referenced in doc_1, doc_18.

data_flows.data: Stream parameters (token, receiver, flowRate, userData), Transaction signature
data_flows.description: User initiates a transaction through their wallet to the CFAv1Forwarder to create a money stream. The Forwarder then calls the CFAv1 contract (ID: 203). Referenced in doc_44, doc_50.
When a stream is created/updated/deleted involving a Super App, the Superfluid Host contract calls the registered callback function on the Super App. Referenced in doc_3, doc_21, doc_24.

data_flows.data: Liquidation transaction parameters (token, sender, receiver), Transaction signature
data_flows.description: A Sentinel node detects an insolvent stream and sends a transaction to the CFAv1 contract (and TOGA contract ID: 215) to liquidate it, claiming the buffer. Referenced in doc_18, doc_19.

data_flows.data: Wrap parameters (amount), ERC20 token approval, Transaction signature
data_flows.description: User approves the Super Token contract to spend their ERC20 tokens and then calls the upgrade/wrap function on the Super Token contract. Referenced in doc_7, doc_55.

data_flows.data: Update units parameters (pool, memberAddress, newUnits, userData), Transaction signature
data_flows.description: Pool Admin calls the GDAv1Forwarder (which then calls GDAv1 Contract ID: 204 or SuperfluidPool Contract Instance ID: 214) to update the distribution units for a pool member. Referenced in doc_30, doc_47.
A user or a client application (like the Superfluid Dashboard ID: 209) sends a GraphQL query to the Superfluid Subgraph to retrieve indexed protocol data. Referenced in doc_48, doc_52, doc_54.
The Vesting Scheduler contract, after a predefined cliff period, automatically initiates a Superfluid stream to the designated recipient by calling the CFAv1 contract. Referenced in doc_17, doc_64.

User calls `runMacro` on the MacroForwarder, which then calls `buildBatchOperations` on a UserDefinedMacro contract (ID: 213). The MacroForwarder then executes these operations via the Superfluid Host (ID: 201). Referenced in doc_45, doc_49.

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet to On-chain Protocol Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user's EOA/wallet (holding private keys) and the Superfluid smart contracts they interact with. Transactions cross this boundary. Referenced in doc_44, doc_50.

trust_boundaries.name: On-chain Protocol to Off-chain Sentinel Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the on-chain Superfluid contracts (specifically CFAv1 and TOGA for liquidations) and the off-chain Sentinel nodes that monitor and trigger transactions. Referenced in doc_18, doc_19.

trust_boundaries.name: Protocol to Governance Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the operational Superfluid smart contracts and the DAO that governs them, making decisions on upgrades and parameters. Referenced in doc_11, doc_41.

trust_boundaries.name: On-chain Protocol to Off-chain Data Indexer Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the Superfluid smart contracts emitting events and the off-chain Subgraph nodes that index this data for querying. Referenced in doc_48, doc_52.

trust_boundaries.name: Super App to Host Callback Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary defining the callback interface where the Superfluid Host contract makes calls to registered Super App contracts upon specific agreement events. Referenced in doc_3, doc_21.

trust_boundaries.name: Client Application to User Wallet Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between a client-side application (like the Superfluid Dashboard or a third-party dApp) and the user's wallet (e.g., MetaMask) for requesting transaction signatures and RPC interactions. Referenced in doc_44, doc_50.

behaviors.name: Manage Money Stream
behaviors.action: User calls createFlow, updateFlow, or deleteFlow (or setFlowrate) on CFAv1Forwarder (which interacts with CFAv1 Contract ID: 203) to manage continuous token payments.
behaviors.description: Users can start, modify, or stop per-second token streams to any receiver. This involves interaction with the CFAv1 contract, typically via the CFAv1Forwarder. Referenced in doc_2, doc_6, doc_34, doc_36, doc_50.

behaviors.name: Manage Token Distribution Pool
behaviors.action: Pool Admin/User creates a pool, updates member units, or initiates a distribution (instant or streamed) via GDAv1Forwarder (which interacts with GDAv1 Contract ID: 204 and SuperfluidPool Contract Instance ID: 214).
behaviors.description: Allows for one-to-many or many-to-many token distributions. Pool Admins manage pools and units; distributors send tokens to the pool for members. Referenced in doc_5, doc_28, doc_29, doc_47.

behaviors.name: Wrap/Unwrap Tokens
behaviors.action: User calls upgrade (wrap) or downgrade (unwrap) on a Super Token contract to convert between underlying ERC20/native assets and Super Tokens.
behaviors.description: Users convert standard ERC20 tokens or native assets into their Super Token equivalents to use Superfluid functionalities, and vice-versa. Referenced in doc_7, doc_14, doc_55.

behaviors.name: Super App Callback Execution
behaviors.action: Superfluid Host contract invokes registered callback functions on a Super App when a relevant agreement event (e.g., stream created to Super App) occurs.
behaviors.description: Super Apps automatically execute custom on-chain logic in response to Superfluid stream or distribution events involving them. Referenced in doc_3, doc_21, doc_24.

behaviors.name: Stream Liquidation
behaviors.action: Sentinel identifies an insolvent stream and calls a liquidation function on the CFAv1 contract (interacting with TOGA Contract ID: 215) to close the stream and claim the buffer.
behaviors.description: Off-chain Sentinels monitor streams and liquidate those where the sender's balance has depleted, ensuring protocol solvency and rewarding the Sentinel. Referenced in doc_1, doc_18, doc_19.

behaviors.name: Automated Token Vesting
behaviors.action: User configures a vesting schedule (amount, duration, cliff) with the Vesting Scheduler, which then automatically creates a Superfluid stream to the recipient.
behaviors.description: Tokens are released to a recipient over time according to a predefined schedule, potentially with a cliff, using Superfluid streams for linear release. Referenced in doc_17, doc_64.

behaviors.name: Execute Batch Transactions via Macro
behaviors.action: User calls `runMacro` on the MacroForwarder, providing parameters for a UserDefinedMacro (ID: 213), to execute multiple Superfluid operations in a single transaction via the Superfluid Host (ID: 201).
behaviors.description: Allows users to combine multiple Superfluid operations (e.g., creating multiple streams, updating pool units) into a single atomic transaction using a macro system. Referenced in doc_45, doc_49.

behaviors.name: Manage Flow Operator Permissions
behaviors.action: User calls functions (e.g., grantPermissions, revokePermissions) on CFAv1 (often via CFAv1Forwarder ID: 206) to manage which accounts (flow operators) can control their streams.
behaviors.description: Users can delegate the ability to create, update, or delete their money streams to other accounts (flow operators) with specified allowances. Referenced in doc_37, doc_51.

behaviors.name: Deploy Super Token
behaviors.action: Developer/User interacts with the Super Token Factory to create a new Wrapper or Pure Super Token, or deploys a custom Super Token contract.
behaviors.description: New Super Tokens (Pure, Wrapper, or Custom) can be deployed to the blockchain, enabling Superfluid functionalities for new or existing assets. Referenced in doc_41, doc_42, doc_43.

behaviors.name: Query Protocol Data
behaviors.action: User or application (e.g. Superfluid Dashboard ID: 209) sends GraphQL queries to the Superfluid Subgraph endpoint to retrieve indexed data about streams, pools, tokens, accounts, etc.
behaviors.description: Allows users and applications to read and analyze on-chain Superfluid protocol data efficiently through a GraphQL API. Referenced in doc_48, doc_52, doc_54.
